dossiers.bear_case: The intraday sell-off after the gap-up could signal distribution by large holders into the beat. Daktronics is cyclical and exposed to capex cuts in sports/venue construction. Competition from lower-cost display manufacturers (Samsung, LG) is ongoing.
dossiers.bull_case: A +69% EPS beat on Daktronics (LED scoreboards, video displays) with 3x confirming volume is a high-quality PEAD setup. The intraday give-back (gap $21.65 → close $19.35) signals institutional digestion is not complete; PEAD drift typically continues 3–5 days after such reversals. Current $20.19 sits $8 below 52-week high ($28.27) — ample room. No analyst coverage pressure at $974M cap means the signal is less priced-in.
dossiers.catalyst: FY2026 Q4 earnings beat: +68.75% EPS surprise (str=0.981) with 3.08x volume anomaly on earnings day. Stock gapped up to $21.65 then closed $19.35 — classic distribution intraday, creating a PEAD re-entry opportunity at $20.19.
dossiers.catalyst_timing: PEAD drift window: 1–5 trading days post-earnings. Earnings reported ~Jun 27.
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: Not fully priced in. The gap-up reversal is consistent with an underreaction being corrected over multiple days. A +69% EPS beat at this cap with 3x volume is rarely absorbed in a single session.

dossiers.bear_case: Stock already moved +42% from pre-earnings levels ($4.85 → $6.88) in three trading days. PEAD requires an initial underreaction to be captured; a +42% post-earnings surge is overreaction territory. Clinical-stage biotech with negative PE (-1.55) carries binary event risk (FDA decisions, Phase 2/3 data readouts). 52-week low was $3.76 just 3 weeks ago — extreme volatility profile.
dossiers.bull_case: Clinical-stage psychedelic pharma with strong pipeline momentum. A 5.11x volume spike and 1.0 earnings-beat strength suggests genuine institutional accumulation. Sector could benefit from continued FDA openness to psychedelic therapies.
dossiers.catalyst: Earnings beat: +23.4% EPS surprise (str=1.0) with 5.11x volume anomaly. Cybin Inc (psychedelic pharma / clinical-stage) reported results that triggered a massive volume event.
dossiers.catalyst_timing: Immediate — but move already substantially complete.
dossiers.disqualify_reason: Catalyst already fully priced: stock moved +42% post-earnings ($4.85 → $6.88). Clinical-stage biotech with binary event risk and negative PE. PEAD opportunity exhausted.
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: Yes. A +42% post-earnings move with 5x volume on a clinical-stage name means the catalyst is fully absorbed. Any remaining PEAD drift is marginal relative to the entry risk.

dossiers.bear_case: The -9.4% pullback from the 52-week high could signal profit-taking by holders who've been sitting on gains. Architectural glass demand is tied to commercial construction — macro headwinds could weigh. Stock has historically been range-bound near $50.
dossiers.bull_case: A +39% EPS beat on a mid-cap industrial with confirming volume is a strong PEAD signal. The -9.4% pullback from the $50.88 intraday high (set on earnings day) offers entry below the market's immediate reaction peak. PEAD drift historically reclaims the initial gap in neglected mid-caps. Apogee's PE of 14.5 is undemanding for the quality of its results.
dossiers.catalyst: FY2026 Q1 earnings beat: +39.0% EPS surprise (str=1.0) with 2.36x volume anomaly. Apogee Enterprises (architectural glass, framing systems) hit a new 52-week high ($50.88) on earnings day then pulled back to $46.10 (-9.4% from high).
dossiers.catalyst_timing: PEAD drift: 1–5 days. Pullback from earnings-day 52-week high creates textbook re-entry point.
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: Partially. The market initially pushed to new highs ($50.88) then gave back ~$4.78. With a +39% EPS beat and volume confirmation, the fundamental case is intact. Entry at $46.10 provides cushion vs the initial reaction peak.

dossiers.bear_case: The short squeeze event already occurred on Jun 24 (210M shares, 10.29x volume). Price has moved from $6.22 (Jun 23 close) to $8.26 (Jun 29) — a +32.7% move. Entering now means chasing an exhausted squeeze. The 52-week low of $6.07 was set Jun 23, one day before the squeeze fired — the setup is resolved. QSR fundamentals are challenged by traffic softness.
dossiers.bull_case: Remaining 29.83% short interest at higher prices could see continued covering pressure. PE of 10.6 is cheap for QSR. If positive fundamental news emerges (e.g., same-store sales beat), trapped shorts could accelerate covering.
dossiers.catalyst: Short squeeze trigger: 29.83% short float with 10.29x volume anomaly. Wendy's experienced a 210M-share volume day on Jun 24 (vs 8.5M baseline), confirming the squeeze already fired.
dossiers.catalyst_timing: Squeeze event occurred Jun 24. Price move is complete.
dossiers.disqualify_reason: Short squeeze catalyst already fired Jun 24 (210M shares, 10.29x volume). Price moved +32.7% from pre-event levels ($6.22 → $8.26). Entering now chases an exhausted move with no new catalyst.
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: Yes. The short-squeeze trigger (10.29x volume day) occurred 5 days ago. The price move (+32.7%) has fully absorbed the squeeze. No new catalyst is identified to restart momentum.

dossiers.bear_case: The +30% overnight gap means the entire PEAD drift is being captured in pre-market on Jun 30. By any Monday entry date, the move will be complete or partially retraced. PEAD requires an initial underreaction; a +30% overnight gap is the opposite. Defense stocks are also sensitive to budget policy shifts.
dossiers.bull_case: AeroVironment is a structural winner in the defense drone market (Ukraine, Taiwan geopolitics). A +24.3% EPS beat reflects continued contract wins. Could sustain momentum beyond initial gap.
dossiers.catalyst: Earnings beat: +24.3% EPS surprise (str=1.0) with 2.21x volume anomaly. AeroVironment (defense drones) reported Q4 results after close Jun 29. Pre-market Jun 30: bid $181 / ask $181.50 vs Jun 29 close of $139.00 — a +30.2% overnight gap.
dossiers.catalyst_timing: Immediate — earnings reported Jun 29. PEAD move is underway RIGHT NOW in pre-market.
dossiers.disqualify_reason: Price gapped +30.2% overnight on earnings catalyst (pre-market $181 bid vs $139 close). PEAD move already in progress — the entire drift is being captured in pre-market before any entry is possible.
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: Yes, dramatically. A +30% pre-market gap on the earnings beat means the market is aggressively pricing the catalyst before regular-hours trading even opens. The PEAD drift opportunity has already closed.

dossiers.bear_case: Clinical-stage oncology company with negative PE (-2.81) and no revenue. Binary data readout risk is high — a negative trial result could crater the stock far below -10% stop. Without a specific imminent catalyst, the squeeze is speculative. Stock is well off its 52-week high, suggesting ongoing fundamental pressure rather than a temporary short squeeze setup.
dossiers.bull_case: 32.1% short float is very high; any positive catalyst (pipeline data, partnership, FDA update) could trigger violent short-covering. Stock at $25.55 vs 52-week high of $44.60 — 74% upside to prior high if squeeze fires. Volume anomaly (2.4x) suggests initial covering pressure is already building.
dossiers.catalyst: Short squeeze setup: 32.1% short float with 2.40x volume anomaly. Zentalis Pharmaceuticals (clinical-stage oncology) seeing elevated short-covering pressure with building volume.
dossiers.catalyst_timing: Near-term; squeeze requires a catalyst to trigger — timeline uncertain.
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: Partially. The 2.4x volume anomaly may reflect early covering, but with 32.1% short interest still outstanding, the bulk of potential covering has not occurred. Risk is that a negative clinical event triggers the inverse move — shorts add rather than cover.

dossiers.bear_case: Modest beat strength (0.738, +5.77% surprise) limits PEAD energy. Office furniture demand is exposed to corporate real estate uncertainty and potential work-from-home re-acceleration. Volume anomaly of 1.88x is at the lower end of meaningful signal.
dossiers.bull_case: MillerKnoll at $20.46 is $2.72 below its 52-week high of $23.18 — 13.3% room to reclaim the high. PE of 15.5 is reasonable for a design/furniture company with an improving earnings trajectory. Return-to-office trends are a structural tailwind. Volume confirmation (1.88x) on an earnings beat is a clean setup.
dossiers.catalyst: Earnings beat: +5.77% EPS surprise (str=0.738) with 1.88x volume anomaly. MillerKnoll (office furniture, Herman Miller / Knoll brands) reported Q4 FY2026 results with volume confirmation.
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: Partially. At $20.46 vs 52-week high of $23.18, current price reflects pre-beat levels. The beat hasn't been fully captured. Moderate beat strength limits how much drift to expect.

dossiers.bear_case: Stock at $13.48 vs 52-week high of $13.78 — already at the ceiling with minimal upside before resistance. Negative PE (-18.59) means no fundamental floor. $169M market cap = thin liquidity, wide bid/ask spreads, high slippage on entry. The 1400% EPS surprise reflects improvement from near-zero (e.g., $0.01 → $0.15 EPS) — not genuine operational scale. Price already moved +32% from pre-earnings levels. Liquidity score penalizes entries at sub-$200M cap.
dossiers.bull_case: Extreme volume (11.65x) confirms some institutional interest. Outdoor/firearms accessories could benefit from seasonal demand. If the company is reaching profitability inflection, the 1400% beat could signal trend reversal.
dossiers.catalyst: Earnings beat: +1400% EPS surprise (str=0.70) with 11.65x volume anomaly. American Outdoor Brands (outdoor/firearms accessories) reported a massive percentage beat on a near-zero earnings base.
dossiers.disqualify_reason: Stock at 52-week high ($13.78) with negative PE (-18.59) and $169M market cap (thin liquidity). 1400% EPS surprise reflects near-zero earnings base. Price already moved +32% pre-entry. Liquidity and slippage risk disqualify at this cap.
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: Yes. At the 52-week high with negative PE and +32% already in, the catalyst is absorbed. The 1400% beat headline obscures a tiny absolute earnings base. Slippage risk on entry in a $169M cap stock is prohibitive.

dossiers.bear_case: Modest beat strength (0.442, +3.71% EPS surprise) limits PEAD energy. At $11B market cap with extensive sell-side coverage, AYI prices earnings efficiently — less PEAD opportunity. Near 52-week high limits upside (3.4% gap to prior ceiling).
dossiers.bull_case: Acuity Brands at $367.62 is only 3.4% below 52-week high ($380.17). Volume confirmation (2.39x) on an earnings beat at $11B cap is notable — requires meaningful institutional flow. PE of 24.0 is fair for a tech-forward building systems company.
dossiers.catalyst: Earnings beat: +3.71% EPS surprise (str=0.442) with 2.39x volume anomaly. Acuity Brands (lighting, building management tech) reported Q3 FY2026 results with volume confirmation.
dossiers.catalyst_timing: PEAD drift: 1–3 days (larger cap, faster digestion).
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: Largely. With full analyst coverage at $11B cap, a modest +3.71% beat has limited capacity to sustain PEAD drift. Weakest PEAD setup in the qualified field.

dossiers.bear_case: Weakest earnings beat in the finalist field (str=0.292, +2.92% EPS surprise). Chemical/adhesives companies face raw material cost volatility. Volume anomaly (1.98x) is near the firing threshold (1.5x). Lowest score in the ranked list — the data is saying this is the least compelling setup.
dossiers.bull_case: H.B. Fuller at $59.14 is 13.8% below 52-week high ($68.63) — room to run. Defensive industrial with diversified end markets. Volume confirmation (1.98x) is moderate but present. PE of 17.6 is fair for a specialty chemical company.
dossiers.catalyst: Earnings beat: +2.92% EPS surprise (str=0.292) with 1.98x volume anomaly. H.B. Fuller (adhesives, sealants, chemical solutions) reported Q2 FY2026 results.
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: Partially. A modest beat with near-threshold volume leaves limited PEAD energy. Not disqualified, but weakest of the 10 finalists.
